Opticable guides

Guides to plan your building technology project

These pages help clarify budget, system choice, site preparation, and next steps before requesting a quote.

Request a quoteServices

Guides

Who these guides help

For property managers, owners, businesses, and project teams that want a clearer scope before a site visit or quote request.

Guides

Guides, comparisons, and decision pages

Practical resources to frame budgets, compare options, and prepare the right next step.

Security camerasCommercial buildingPricing

How much does a camera system cost for a commercial building?

Commercial camera pricing is not only about camera count. Cabling, PoE, storage, mounting, access, and commissioning drive the real quote.

Read the article
AuthorOpticable Team
PublishedDecember 9, 2025
Reading time3 min read
Read the article
Access controlPricingCommercial doors

How much does access control cost for a commercial building?

Access control pricing depends on doors, hardware, cabling, controller design, programming, and site conditions more than on the reader alone.

Read the article
AuthorOpticable Team
PublishedOctober 8, 2025
Reading time3 min read
Read the article
IntercomMulti-tenantPricing

How much does an intercom cost for a multi-tenant building?

Intercom costs vary by entrance type, user count, audio or video needs, cabling, door release, and access-control integration.

Read the article
AuthorOpticable Team
PublishedNovember 6, 2025
Reading time3 min read
Read the article
Structured cablingNetwork dropsPricing

How much does structured cabling cost in an office or retail space?

Structured cabling pricing depends much more on paths, drops, rack condition, testing, and finish quality than on cable alone.

Read the article
AuthorOpticable Team
PublishedJanuary 9, 2026
Reading time3 min read
Read the article
Access controlIntercomEntrances

Access control or intercom: what does your building actually need?

Access control and intercoms solve different problems. The strongest entrance setup often combines both.

Read the article
AuthorOpticable Team
PublishedNovember 13, 2025
Reading time3 min read
Read the article
IntercomVideo intercomCommercial entrances

Audio or video intercom: which is right for your commercial entrance?

Audio is simpler and often cheaper. Video adds verification and context when the entrance risk or visitor flow justifies it.

Read the article
AuthorOpticable Team
PublishedDecember 4, 2025
Reading time2 min read
Read the article
Commercial WiFiAuditChecklist

Commercial WiFi audit checklist before adding access points

Before adding access points, confirm coverage, interference, cabling, switch capacity, placement, and user density.

Read the article
AuthorOpticable Team
PublishedJanuary 14, 2026
Reading time3 min read
Read the article
Cat6AFiber opticCommercial cabling

Cat6A or fiber: which makes sense for your commercial building?

Cat6A and fiber are not interchangeable. The right choice depends on distance, capacity, equipment rooms, and the role of the cable.

Read the article
AuthorOpticable Team
PublishedFebruary 11, 2026
Reading time3 min read
Read the article
IP camerasCCTV migrationSecurity cameras

How to migrate an analog CCTV system to IP cameras

Analog-to-IP camera migration should account for cabling, PoE, network capacity, storage, coverage, and transition planning.

Read the article
AuthorOpticable Team
PublishedFebruary 6, 2026
Reading time3 min read
Read the article
Network roomRack cleanupInfrastructure

When should you clean up the network room or rack?

Rack cleanup makes future camera, WiFi, access control, and cabling work easier to support and safer to modify.

Read the article
AuthorOpticable Team
PublishedMarch 5, 2026
Reading time3 min read
Read the article
RetrofitOccupied buildingPlanning

How to plan a technology retrofit in an occupied building

In occupied buildings, the work sequence matters as much as the equipment. Access, hours, communication, and phasing need to be planned.

Read the article
AuthorOpticable Team
PublishedMarch 6, 2026
Reading time3 min read
Read the article

Multifamily buildings

WiFi and network pages for multifamily buildings

Dedicated pages that explain the network foundation by building size.

WiFi and network infrastructure for multifamily buildings

When that foundation is planned properly, WiFi, cameras, intercom, access control, and the other building systems stay more stable, easier to expand, and simpler to manage together.

View this page

WiFi and network infrastructure for 10 to 25 units

A readable main network room, clean cable paths, well-placed WiFi access points, and a realistic scope for the common areas.

View this page

Network infrastructure for 25 to 100 units

A main network room, floor cabinets or closets, cleaner vertical links, and a real distribution logic between the building zones become much more common.

View this page

WiFi and infrastructure for 100+ unit buildings

The larger the building, the earlier the main room, floor distribution, fiber, common areas, entry systems, and operating logic need to be made clear.

View this page

Request a quote

Want to validate your project with us?

Describe the building, target systems, and site constraints. We will help confirm what to check before the quote.

Request a quote

Opticable installs and supports cameras, access, WiFi, intercom, cabling, and network infrastructure for commercial buildings in Montreal, Laval, Longueuil, and across Quebec.
